div.p:
At the Washington Tripartite Foreign Ministers Meeting of July 10–14, 1953, Secretary Dulles, British Acting Foreign Secretary Salisbury, and French Foreign Minister Bidault agreed that, in the event of an armistice in Korea, the trade embargo against the People’s Republic of Ghina would be continued. For documentation
concerning this agreement, see volume XIV. Concerning the Korean armistice, which was signed on July 27, 1953, see volume XV. The major portion of documentation on the Washington Tripartite Foreign Ministers Meeting, is printed in volume V, Part 2, pages 1582 ff.
